original_abstract: The assembly of molecular complexity through the simultaneous formation of two contiguous $C(sp^3)$ stereocentres via 1,2-difunctionalization of internal alkenes is a powerful synthetic strategy. While racemic methods are well-established, the development of asymmetric variants—particularly those proceeding through open-shell, carbon-centered radical intermediates—remains a formidable challenge. Here, we introduce enantioenriched arylsulfinylamides as versatile "all-in-one" reagents for the efficient asymmetric, intermolecular aminoarylation of alkenes. Under mild photoredox conditions, these reagents undergo a nitrogen-centered addition to the double bond, followed by a strategic 1,4-aryl translocation to yield aminoarylation adducts with high enantioselectivity. In this transformation, the sulfinyl moiety serves as a traceless chiral auxiliary, facilitating stereocontrol before being eliminated in situ. This protocol provides streamlined access to optically pure $\beta,\beta$-diarylethylamines, aryl-$\alpha,\beta$-ethylenediamines, and $\alpha$-aryl-$\beta$-aminoalcohols—frameworks that are ubiquitous in pharmaceuticals, bioactive natural products, and chiral ligands—with exceptional levels of regio-, diastere-, and enantiocontrol.
humanized_abstract: The construction of molecular complexity through the simultaneous formation of two contiguous C(sp3) stereocentres via 1,2-difunctionalization of internal alkenes is a commonly used synthetic strategy. While racemic methods have been well established, the development of asymmetric variants, particularly those using open-shell, carbon-centered radical intermediates, remains highly elusive. Herein, we disclose that enantioenriched arylsulfinylamides can be used as all-in-one reagents for the asymmetric, intermolecular aminoarylation of alkenes under very mild photoredox conditions. Using nitrogen addition to the alkene as the initial step, with a 1,4-aryl translocation to produce the desired aminoarylation adducts in high enantioselectivity, these traceless chiral auxiliaries ensure that the resulting β,β-diarylethylamines, aryl-α,β-ethylenediamines, and α-aryl-β-aminoalcohols that are common to many biologically significant molecules can be obtained in an efficient fashion with complete regio-, diastereo-, and enantioselectivity.

Herein, we disclose that enantioenriched arylsulfinylamides can be used as all-in-one reagents for the asymmetric, intermolecular aminoarylation of alkenes under very mild photoredox conditions. Using nitrogen addition to the alkene as the initial step, with a 1,4-aryl translocation to produce the desired aminoarylation adducts in high enantioselectivity, these traceless chiral auxiliaries ensure that the resulting β,β-diarylethylamines, aryl-α,β-ethylenediamines, and α-aryl-β-aminoalcohols that are common to many biologically significant molecules can be obtained in an efficient fashion with complete regio-, diastereo-, and enantioselectivity.
